ddt:Target.Name: 4RD_SUV_V4
ddt:Target.Description:
4RD_SUV_V3 - Update of database Creator: Roxana IONESCU Modified bus off DTC from C055 to C076
4RD_SUV_V2 - Update of database Creator: Roxana IONESCU Modifications according to diag file shared by supplier: 4WS- Diag v5.8- IDOC-2017-00077494_AI_20211007 -DID modifications -DTC modifications -Added IOcontrol -Added Routines -Auto-identification update
4RD_SUV_V1 - Base version Creator : Priyanivetha THANGAMALAI
2022-1-4 Identification added by tool (CORP\p111373) => DiagVersion=4, Supplier=A32 , Soft=4400 , Version=0000
2022-4-13 Identification added by tool (CORP\k141500) => DiagVersion=4, Supplier=A32 , Soft=4500 , Version=SW4_V050_C004

Item.Text: No possible classification about the type of fault
Open circuit or Short circuit to ground
Open circuit or Short circuit to battery
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures
Device and failure type ODB codding
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adjustment failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or system operation obstructed or blocked
4WS FAULT -Motor Driver Circuit 1 Power Circuit Relay
Battery Voltage Fault - Wire harness or ECU or Battery
Motor position sensor fault - Excitation/Induction circuit failure
Rear Wheel Angle Request Out of range
Rear Wheel Angle Request plausibility failure

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Item.Text: Control aborted due to vehicle speed > 0km/h (12h)
Control aborted due to tool unplugged (session C0 stopped) (14h)

Data.Name: VehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber_R
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM

Data.Name: Rear Wheel Angle Position Request_Short List
Rear Wheel Angle Position Request_Long List
SWA_EPS_Plausibility.EPS Checksum or CRC failure flag
CDM Functional Status.Vehicle Speed Gradient failure flag

Item.Text: no vehicle speed gradient failure detected

Data.Name: CDM Functional Status.Vehicle Speed Clock failure flag

Item.Text: no vehicle speed clock failure detected

Data.Name: CDM Functional Status.Vehicle Speed Checksum or CRC failure flag

Item.Text: no vehicle speed checksum failure detected

Data.Name: CDM Functional Status.SWA Gradient failure flag
CDM Functional Status.SWA Clock failure flag
CDM Functional Status.SWA Checksum or CRC failure flag

Item.Text: Sleep or Crank fault, Disable DiagMuxDTC
All Power Up tests are completed and succesful
ECU Temperature is less than TmaxECU
ECU Temperature is greater than TmaxECU
RWA Request CAN frame absent or invalid
RWA Request CAN frame absent or invalid
RWA order CAN frame absent or invalid
RWA Order CAN frame absent or invalid
Absent CAN value of Engine States
ECU temperature is less than TcriticalECU
ECU temperature is greater than TcriticalECU
Routine completed and Nok : stopped due to vehicle speed > 2km/h or 4WS controlled by VMC
Routine completed and NOK : stopped due to internal fault
routine completed and NOK : routine stopped due to diag tool unplugged (session C0 stopped)
Routine completed and NOK : Return to 0 stopped due to internal fault
Routine completed and NOK : routine stopped due to diag tool unplugged (session C0 stopped)
Control stopped by rear wheel angle request

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Comment: Name and address information of the supplier.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier name and address information.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: diagnosticVersion
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifier
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Data.Name: Routine Control Subfunction
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifier Recived data
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Data.Name: Copy RoutineIdentifier
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Item.Text: Routine completed and OK: No internal failure
Routine completed and NOK: Vehicle speed>2km/h or 4WS controlled by VMC
Routine completed and NOK: Internal fault
Routine completed and NOK: diag tool unpluged

Data.Name: Max Value for actuator oscillation _while in start routine
Speed of actuator for oscillation routine

Item.Text: Routine completed and Nok : stopped due to vehicle speed > 0km/h
Routine completed and Nok : stopped due to failure
routine completed and NOK : routine stopped due to diag tool unplugged (session C0 stopped)
Routine completed and Nok : RAZ stopped due to internal fault
Routine completed and NOK : routine stopped due to diag tool unplugged (session C0 stopped)

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSnapshot
Request.Comment: Requete non utilisable dans les outils standard de DDT2000

Request.Name: ClearDiagnosticInformation.All
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Request.Name: RoutineControl
Request.Comment: You should remove ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.xxx data. These are examples.

DataItem.Name: Max Value for actuator oscillation _while in start routine
Speed of actuator for oscillation routine

Device.Name: Motor position sensor fault - Excitation/Induction circuit failure
4WS FAULT -Motor Driver Circuit 1 Power Circuit Relay

Test.Description: Motor or Driver or Current sensor Open circuit
Motor or Driver or Current sensor short circuit
Motor or Driver or Current sensor Open circuit
4WS Fault- Temperature sensor in ECU stuck low/high
4WS Fault- Temperature sensor in ECU/Abnormal temperature rise (Low or Hgh level rise)
4WS Fault- Temperature sensor in ECU/Abnormal temperature rise (Low or High level rise)

Device.Name: Battery Voltage Fault - Wire harness or ECU or Battery

Test.Description: Power supply to ECU - Voltage drop (Protection mode), Abnormal voltage drop (Failure mode)
IG voltage monitor stuck high (Loss APC)
Power supply to ECU - Voltage rise (Protection mode), Abnormal voltage rise (Failure mode)

Device.Name: Rear Wheel Angle Request plausibility failure

Test.Description: General CAN network failure - Rear Wheel Angle Request Plausibility

Device.Name: Rear Wheel Angle Request Out of range

Test.Description: General CAN network failure - Rear Wheel Angle Request Out of Range

Label.Text: Valeurs des paramètres à ecrire en mémoire
A = Janv, B = Fev , C = March
D = April, E = May , F = June
G = July, H = Aug , I = Sept
J = Oct, K = Nov , L = Dec
Avant de lancer la routine (bouton start), il faut placer le véhicule sur un pont roues arrières pendantes
Reset ECU permet : prise en compte d'une calibration ou réactivation du diagnostic après un clear diag
Le reset calculateur ne s'effectuera qu'à vitesse nulle :

Screen.Name: Read/ Write - Tuning/VIN - Scenario error - Scenario error

Label.Text: Valeurs des paramètres à ecrire en mémoire (Vitesse <2km/h!)
Valeurs des paramètres à ecrire en mémoire (Vitesse <2km/h!)
